member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Configuration.FileSystemEditorConfigOptionsProvider
member.summary:
Reads .editorconfig files from the file system and resolves the merged settings that apply to a given document path, following the standard EditorConfig upward-search semantics (root = true stops the walk).
Parsed .editorconfig files are cached by path and last-write time. Call when a file changes so the next lookup re-reads it.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Discovery.CSharpBindingDiscoveryService
member.summary: Drives Roslyn/C# source-level binding discovery when a .cs step-definition file is opened or edited. Resolves all projects that own the document via the membership index (), parses the supplied source text with (via ), and patches each owning project's so the change is reflected immediately without waiting for a build / connector run.
member.remarks: Invariant I2 — if the membership index has received a baseline and the file is not in it, the file is excluded from all registries and this method is a no-op for it. This prevents phantom bindings from open-but-excluded files.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.CodeLens.RefreshCodeLensParams
member.summary: Payload for the reqnroll/refreshCodeLens server-to-client notification.
member.remarks: The server pushes this to the Visual Studio client after a full binding-registry replacement (startup connector discovery, post-build, or membership-baseline arrival) so the VS client can invalidate its already-rendered C# step code lenses and re-pull fresh usage counts. This is the VS equivalent of the standard workspace/codeLens/refresh request, which VS cannot route to our pipe-based code-lens provider. Other IDE clients use workspace/codeLens/refresh instead and ignore this notification.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Definition.GoToHooksHandler
member.summary:
Handles the custom reqnroll/goToHooks request (Hook Navigation — "Go to Hooks").
Given a cursor position in a .feature file, returns all hook bindings that are applicable at that position, filtered by context level (Feature / Scenario / Step) and any tag/scope expressions on the hook.
A separate custom message is used rather than reusing textDocument/definition because that message is already used by Go to Step Definition on step lines; the server cannot distinguish the two intents from position alone, and step-level hooks ([BeforeStep] / [AfterStep]) would be unreachable.
Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Definition.GoToStepDefinitionsHandler
member.summary:
Handles the custom reqnroll/goToStepDefinitions request (Go to Step Definition).
Returns all step-definition bindings that match the step at the queried cursor position, each with its source location and metadata (step keyword type, qualified method name). This richer payload lets the VS extension's picker display labels such as "[When] CalculatorSteps.AddNumbers (Steps.cs:18)" rather than just "Steps.cs:18".
The standard textDocument/definition handler () is retained for generic LSP clients that do not understand this custom message.
Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentActivated.DocumentActivatedHandler
member.summary: Handles reqnroll/documentActivated (issue #85): forces a fresh binding-match recompute and diagnostics/semantic-tokens republish for a document the VS extension has just detected becoming the active tab, independent of whatever normally triggers that (didOpen/didChange, a binding-registry replacement, a config change).
member.remarks:
Exists as a backstop for the #78 class of startup race: several .feature documents can be opened back-to-back before the server has finished discovery, leaving some of them with stale or incomplete diagnostics that nothing subsequently retries. Reusing + — the exact same pipeline uses for didOpen/didChange — means diagnostics, semantic tokens, and any future subscriber of the notification all get refreshed for free, with no separate republish logic to maintain here.
Safe to call for a document the server does not know is open: ParseAsync no-ops when there is no buffer for the URI, and the notification then carries a synthetic version of 0 — this deliberately degrades to nothing happening rather than throwing, since the VS-side activation signal can race ahead of didOpen (see #85 design discussion).

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentActivated.DocumentActivatedHandler.HandleAsync(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentActivated.DocumentActivatedParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles a reqnroll/documentActivated notification to force a match recompute.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentActivated.DocumentActivatedParams
member.summary: Parameters for the reqnroll/documentActivated notification (issue #85).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentOutline.FeatureDocumentSymbolHandler
member.summary: Handles textDocument/documentSymbol for .feature files (Document Outline). Returns a nested hierarchy (Feature → Background / Rule → Scenario / Scenario Outline → Step / Examples) when the client declared hierarchicalDocumentSymbolSupport, or a flattened list otherwise (see remarks).
member.remarks: Visual Studio's own LSP client does not declare hierarchicalDocumentSymbolSupport in its documentSymbol capability (confirmed by inspector-log capture: only {"dynamicRegistration":true}, no hierarchical flag) — per the LSP spec, a server MUST NOT send nested to such a client and must fall back to flat . Prior to this fix the handler always sent nested regardless of client capability, which is the most likely reason VS's built-in Document Outline window / Breadcrumb Bar stayed empty despite VS successfully calling documentSymbol and getting a 200 response: VS's typed client presumably fails to deserialize a response shaped as (range/selectionRange/children) against the flat (location/containerName) shape it declared support for. VS Code/Rider are unaffected — they do declare hierarchical support.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentOutline.FeatureDocumentSymbolHandler.GetRegistrationOptions(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Client.Capabilities.DocumentSymbolCapability,OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Client.Capabilities.ClientCapabilities)
member.summary: Gets or sets the get registration options.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentOutline.FeatureDocumentSymbolHandler.Handle(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.DocumentSymbolParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles a textDocument/documentSymbol request for the feature-outline tree.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.DocumentOutline.FeatureDocumentSymbolHandler.HandleHierarchicalAsync(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.DocumentSymbolParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles the custom reqnroll/documentSymbolHierarchical request: always returns the nested shape, regardless of what the real LSP client (VS) has declared support for via GetRegistrationOptions.
member.remarks: The VS extension's own Navigation Bar (Issue #5 / Navigation Bar symbol source design, Option B) fetches symbols through this method rather than textDocument/documentSymbol. It parses the response itself (GherkinNavigationBarSymbolService), so it isn't affected by VS's declared client capability the way is — but 's capability-driven shape is per-handler-instance, not per-request, so without a separate method the Nav Bar's own fetch would silently receive the flattened shape too (and its range/children parsing, written for the nested shape, would break) whenever VS's client declares no hierarchical support.

member.remarks: Registered manually (see LanguageServerOptionsExtensions.InitializeCustomProtocolRouting) with foldingRangeProvider declared statically in the initialize response (see Program.ConfigureServer), instead of via OmniSharp's dynamic-registration handler interface. vscode-languageclient's dynamic client/registerCapability round trip for inlayHint/foldingRange races VS Code's restore of previously-open .feature tabs on window load — if the tab renders first, VS Code never re-checks for a provider for the rest of the session. Static declaration removes the race entirely.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.InlayHints.FeatureInlayHintHandler
member.summary: Handles textDocument/inlayHint for .feature files (F23 — binding info hints). Shows the bound step definition's method name at the end of each step line, with the full signature in the hint's tooltip. Ambiguous steps show a match count instead; a Scenario Outline/Background step whose example rows resolve to more than one distinct binding shows a binding count. Undefined steps get no hint — the diagnostic already covers those.
member.remarks: Registered manually (see LanguageServerOptionsExtensions.InitializeCustomProtocolRouting) with inlayHintProvider declared statically in the initialize response (see Program.ConfigureServer), instead of via OmniSharp's dynamic-registration handler interface. vscode-languageclient's dynamic client/registerCapability round trip for inlayHint/foldingRange races VS Code's restore of previously-open .feature tabs on window load — if the tab renders first, VS Code never re-checks for a provider for the rest of the session. Static declaration removes the race entirely.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.References.FindStepUsagesHandler
member.summary:
Handles the custom reqnroll/findStepUsages request.
Implements MediatR IRequestHandler to allow automatic routing via AddMediatR, avoiding the need for manual OnRequest delegate registration and IServiceProvider capture. Implements the full three-state contract that textDocument/references cannot carry:
Returns when the caret is not on any step-definition binding (client falls through to the built-in C# Find All References).
Returns a response with isBinding=true and an empty location list when the binding has no matching feature steps ("0 usages" window).
Returns a response with isBinding=true and populated locations otherwise.
Each location includes a stepText field extracted directly from the document snapshot stored in the match cache at parse time — the client does not need to read the file from disk.
member.remarks: Shares the same dependency set as ; both are registered in Program.cs and resolved as singletons from the DI container.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.References.FindStepUsagesHandler.HandleAsync(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.ReferenceParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles a reqnroll/findStepUsages request. Request params are identical to textDocument/references ().

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.References.FindStepUsagesResponse
member.summary:
Response DTO for the custom reqnroll/findStepUsages request (Find Step Definition Usages / Find All References, phase 2b).
Carries the full three-state contract that textDocument/references cannot express because OmniSharp's LocationOrLocationLinks serializer cannot return null:
= → caret is not on a binding; client falls through to built-in C# FAR.
= , empty → binding present, 0 usages; client shows "0 usages".
= , non-empty → the matching feature-file steps.
Note: the handler never returns JSON null — OmniSharp's OnRequest framework sends an error response for null returns from custom-method handlers instead of serialising JSON null, so = is used as the sentinel.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.References.FindStepUsagesResponse.IsBinding
member.summary: when the queried position is on a step-definition binding. when the position is not on a binding — the client should fall through to built-in C# Find All References.

member.summary: The feature-file steps that match this binding. Empty when is but no steps match ("0 usages").

member.summary:
Handles textDocument/references requests originating from a cursor position in a .cs binding file (Find Step Definition Usages / Find All References).
Implements MediatR IRequestHandler to allow automatic routing via AddMediatR, avoiding the need for manual OnRequest delegate registration and IServiceProvider capture.
member.remarks: Primary-owner resolution / shared-feature scoping 2B: the scope is restricted to the projects that own the queried .cs file. This prevents cross-project bleed when two projects have step definitions at the same source location (same file name + line in a shared binding class).

member.summary: The bare step expression as written in the source attribute (e.g. the first number is {int}), without the step-type prefix or scope suffix. This is what the rename dialog should seed so the user edits the live expression form (preserving Cucumber parameter types) rather than a regex projection. Falls back to the registry expression when the source literal cannot be resolved.

member.summary: Handles textDocument/prepareRename, textDocument/rename, reqnroll/renameTargets, and reqnroll/selectRenameTarget for the Step Rename refactoring feature.
Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.StepRenameHandler.HandlePrepareRenameAsync(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.PrepareRenameParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Validates that the cursor is on a renameable binding. Returns the range of the renameable text (attribute string or step text) — for .feature files, paired with a carrying the binding's abstract expression (e.g. "the second number is {int}") instead of the concrete step text — or null if rename is not available at this position.
member.remarks: Seeding the rename box with the abstract expression, rather than the concrete text literally in the buffer, is deliberate (issue #33 follow-up): a spec-compliant client has no buffer text to anchor a partial in-place edit against when the placeholder text doesn't appear in the document, so it can only submit the box's full edited content as newName — turning an inherently ambiguous "did the user edit the wording, the parameter value, or an arbitrary fragment?" problem into an unambiguous one: newName is always the complete new abstract expression.

member.summary: Computes the LSP range of a string literal's inner text, i.e. its span with the surrounding quote characters excluded (2 leading characters for a verbatim string's @", 1 otherwise; 1 trailing character for the closing ").

member.summary: Finds all bindings that match the feature step at the given cursor position by querying the binding match cache for the owning projects.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.StepRenameHandler.FindBindingsAtFeatureStep(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.DocumentUri,System.String,OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.Position,OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.Range@)
member.summary: Finds all bindings that match the feature step at the given cursor position, and the matched step's own text span (excluding the keyword/indentation) via . Callers that only need the range for editing (prepareRename must offer exactly the text that HandleRenameAsync will later replace at usage.Range — otherwise the keyword/indentation the client seeds the dialog with gets duplicated when the edit is applied) should use this overload.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.StepRenameHandler.HandleSelectRenameTargetAsync(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.SelectRenameTargetParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles reqnroll/selectRenameTarget — stores the selected attribute for the next textDocument/rename call.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.StepRenameHandler.FindAttributeLiteralAsync(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.DocumentUri,Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Core.Bindings.ProjectStepDefinitionBinding)
member.summary: Resolves the string-literal attribute argument for by its SOURCE LOCATION, not by matching the registry's expression text. The registry expression is a discovery-time projection (a Cucumber expression is rendered to a regex during discovery, and it reflects the last compiled build rather than the live buffer), so it cannot be relied on to equal the raw attribute string literal. Line drift from a stale build is tolerated by choosing the nearest candidate method.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.StepRenameHandler.ShortenMethodQualifier(System.String)
member.summary: Keeps only the last two dot-segments of a fully qualified method name (class + method), dropping the namespace. Two ambiguous bindings from the same project usually share the same namespace prefix, so keeping it just wastes the picker's limited width without helping the user distinguish the entries.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.Rename.StepRenameHandler.ReconcileParameterTokens(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Rebuilds so that its parameter slots carry the exact tokens from (positionally). This keeps the original parameter syntax — a Cucumber {int} stays {int}, a regex (.*) stays (.*) — even when the rename dialog seeded a different projection. The user's edits to the non-parameter text are preserved. When the slot counts differ, the user's text is honoured verbatim.

member.summary: Returns the first string-literal argument of every attribute on that is a step-definition attribute for (Given/When/ Then, or StepDefinition which applies to all step kinds).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.PublishSemanticTokensParams
member.summary: Payload for the reqnroll/semanticTokens server-to-client notification.
member.remarks: The server pushes this to the Visual Studio client whenever a feature file's tokens change, because VS's built-in LSP semantic-token colorizer cannot map Reqnroll's custom token types and does not reliably pull tokens. The VS client decodes with the legend from the initialize response and colours the file via its own classifier. Other IDE clients ignore this notification and use the standard pull-based semantic tokens flow.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.PublishSemanticTokensParams.Uri
member.summary: The document URI these tokens apply to.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.PublishSemanticTokensParams.Version
member.summary: The document version the tokens were encoded from.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.PublishSemanticTokensParams.Data
member.summary: The LSP semantic-token data (flat 5-int relative encoding).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.ReqnrollSemanticTokens
member.summary: The fixed Reqnroll semantic-token legend and the →token mapping.

remarks.para: The server advertises one shared set of custom semantic token types whose names (reqnroll.keyword, reqnroll.tag, …) match the custom ClassificationTypeDefinition names used by the existing Reqnroll Visual Studio extension (DeveroomClassifications). The legend is identical for every IDE client; it is the client's responsibility to map each legend name to a concrete editor colour:

list.item: Visual Studio re-uses the DeveroomClassifications MEF exports, so each legend name resolves to the classification of the same name.
VS Code maps the names via semanticTokenScopes / configurationDefaults.
Rider registers a TextAttributesKey per name and maps via the LSP descriptor.

remarks.para: Because the legend no longer varies per IDE, there is no profile abstraction or --ide-based selection here: the definition is a single static contract. No token modifiers are used — each Reqnroll concept is its own classification/colour, so the distinction is carried entirely by the token type name.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.ReqnrollSemanticTokens.Legend
member.summary: The token-type legend advertised to every IDE client in the initialize response.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.ReqnrollSemanticTokens.TryGetToken(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Core.Parsing.Gherkin.DeveroomTag,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Maps a to a zero-based index into token types and a modifier bitmask.
member.returns: if the tag should produce a semantic token; if it should be silently skipped (block/container tags and unclassified artifacts).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.SemanticTokenService
member.summary: Maps instances to LSP semantic token integer tuples on demand and caches the encoded result per document version. Encoding is deferred until the client sends a semantic tokens request. The token type legend and the tag→token mapping are the fixed Reqnroll definitions in (identical for every IDE client).

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.SemanticTokenService.InvalidateCache(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.DocumentUri)
member.summary: Evicts the cached token result for so that the next call re-encodes from the current tags. Must be called whenever stores a new tag set for a document whose version has not changed (e.g. after binding discovery completes for an already-open file).

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.SemanticTokens.SemanticTokenService.Encode(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Core.Parsing.Gherkin.DeveroomTag})
member.summary: Converts a flat collection of instances into the LSP semantic token integer encoding (5 ints per token): deltaLine, deltaStartChar, length, tokenTypeIndex, tokenModifierBitset. Only leaf-level tags that map to a token type are emitted; container block tags (FeatureBlock, etc.) are not emitted themselves but their children are processed recursively.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Features.TextSync.ICSharpFileTextCache
member.summary: Tracks the current (possibly unsaved) text of every .cs file the client has open, independent of — which is deliberately Gherkin-only and never populated for .cs documents (see TextDocumentSyncHandler).
member.remarks: Without this, any code that needs a .cs file's live content (e.g. Step Rename refactoring locating the attribute literal to edit, or the Roslyn/C# source-level binding discovery's incremental Roslyn reconciliation) had no source but disk, which is stale for any unsaved edit — whether that edit came from the user typing directly in the file or from this server's own Step Rename refactoring applying its own edit. TextDocumentSyncHandler populates this on every .cs didOpen/ didChange regardless of source, and removes it on didClose — once closed, disk is the source of truth again (VS prompts to save or discard before a close reaches the server).

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Hosting.LanguageServerOptionsExtensions.CamelCaseSerializer
member.summary: 's parameterless overload serializes with Newtonsoft's global default settings — PascalCase property names, not the lowercase camelCase every other LSP payload on the wire uses. That silently produced e.g. "Start"/"Character" instead of "start"/"character" for plain ranges, tolerated by clients doing case-insensitive property matching — but it breaks 's union-type discrimination outright: its converter looks for the exact lowercase keys "range"/"placeholder"/ "defaultBehavior" to tell which variant is present, and silently produces neither on the read side when the write side used PascalCase (issue #33 follow-up).

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Hosting.LanguageServerOptionsExtensions.InitializeCustomProtocolRouting(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Server.LanguageServerOptions)
member.summary:
Seeds workspace scopes and configures custom Reqnroll protocol routing.
Because OmniSharp's LSP parameter types (e.g., ReferenceParams, SemanticTokensParams) do not implement MediatR's IRequest, we must use OnRequest/OnNotification delegates for custom method routing. This method encapsulates the service resolution in a strongly-typed resolver initialized during OnStarted, eliminating nullable IServiceProvider captures and null-forgiving operators.

remarks.para: Logging split (issue #84): app-level code in this server (handlers, discovery, workspace/document services, etc.) logs exclusively through the DI-registered singleton (), consumed via the LogInfo/LogWarning/LogError/... extension methods in — this is deliberate and should stay the pattern for new app-level code, rather than switching individual classes to ILogger<T>.

Separately, ILogger<T> () is what OmniSharp's own internals log through — request dispatch, DryIoc, JSON-RPC plumbing. That pipeline is established by 's options.ConfigureLogging(...) call (SetMinimumLevel, AddLanguageProtocolLogging, ) directly into options.Services, gated by its own --protocol-log-level and writing to a dedicated reqnroll-*-protocol-*.log file via the shared — deliberately a separate store from the app-level "server" log. Do not re-register ILoggerFactory/ILogger<> anywhere else in this DI container (e.g. in ) — a later registration wins the last-registration-wins resolution and silently replaces this one, which previously caused every OmniSharp-internal message to leak into the app-level "server" log at whatever --log-level happened to be, instead of its own file gated by --protocol-log-level.
The three log-level dials below (--log-level, --protocol-log-level, --trace) remain intentionally independent — see each parameter's remarks on .

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Hosting.Program.ConfigureServer(OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Server.LanguageServerOptions,System.String,System.Diagnostics.TraceLevel,OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.Models.InitializeTrace,System.Diagnostics.TraceLevel)
member.summary: Applies the full server configuration (logging, DI graph, capabilities, custom notifications) to . The transport (input/output) is intentionally NOT set here so that callers can choose it: production uses stdio (see ); the in-process protocol specs host the server over an in-memory pipe.
member.param: The --ide identifier of the connecting client (e.g. "visualstudio"), or when absent. Currently unused by the semantic-token pipeline (the legend is shared across IDEs); retained for features that may vary behaviour per IDE.
The --log-level verbosity requested by the client, defaulting to . Drives only the file-backed (our own app-level logging) — deliberately independent of .

The --protocol-log-level verbosity for OmniSharp's own internal diagnostics, defaulting to . Drives the OmniSharp protocol-logging minimum level, which feeds both the standard window/logMessage notification (AddLanguageProtocolLogging(), visible to the client) and (a dedicated reqnroll-*-protocol-*.log file, so that content survives even if the client's Output panel is never inspected). Independent of — see that parameter's remarks.

member.summary: F41: resolves the trace level to apply at the initialize handshake. (InitializeParams.Trace) wins whenever the client actually asked for something; there is indistinguishable from "the client didn't set this field", so it must not clobber (the --trace command-line default).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Hosting.ProjectPreloadListener
member.summary: Listens on a process-local named pipe for reqnroll/projectLoaded and reqnroll/projectFiles payloads sent by IDE glue before the LSP initialize handshake completes, and dispatches them directly to .
member.remarks: OmniSharp's LanguageServer.Initialize() blocks until the client's real initialize request is received, and defers/queues any other request or notification routed through its own JSON-RPC dispatcher until then. This listener bypasses that dispatcher entirely — it is started against LanguageServer.PreInit(...).Services, which is fully constructed (DI container built, resolvable) before Initialize() is ever called. See 's own "auto-creating workspace scope" comment — the workspace/project model was already designed to tolerate project notifications arriving before initialize's workspace folders exist.

member.summary: Accepts preload connections until is cancelled (the caller cancels once the real initialize handshake completes — the side channel has no further purpose after that point).
member.param: Defaults to of the current process; overridable for test isolation.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Hosting.ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddReqnrollLspHandlers(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary:
Registers OmniSharp LSP protocol handlers as singletons.
NOTE: MediatR notification handlers (e.g., SemanticTokensRefreshHandler, DiagnosticsPublishHandler) are auto-discovered and registered as transients by the AddMediatR call in ConfigureServer. DO NOT add explicit AddSingleton<INotificationHandler<T>> registrations here, as it will cause MediatR to dispatch every notification to two handler instances (the transient from the scan and the singleton from the explicit call).
The handlers listed below ARE registered explicitly as singletons because OmniSharp resolves them from the root DryIoc container (not from a per-request scope), and they hold injected ILanguageServer references that must live for the server's lifetime.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Logging.LspIdeSupportLogger
member.summary:
used by the LSP server process. Delegates to a composed of:
— writes to output
— appends to the Reqnroll log file
Emits a session-start banner as the first log line so runs within a day-appended file can be distinguished by version, PID, and server path.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Logging.LspIdeSupportLogger.#ctor(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Hosting.ClientIdeContext)
member.summary: Creates the composite logger for the current client IDE and logs a session-start banner.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Logging.ProtocolLoggerProvider
member.summary: Bridges OmniSharp's own internal diagnostics (request dispatch, DryIoc, JSON-RPC plumbing — whatever the library itself logs via ILogger<T>) into a dedicated Reqnroll log file, in addition to the existing window/logMessage notification (AddLanguageProtocolLogging()) that only reaches the connected client.
member.remarks: Without this, OmniSharp-internal diagnostics exist only as a transient client notification — if the user doesn't think to copy their editor's Output panel, that information is gone by the time we're asked to look at a bug report. Writes to a separate reqnroll-*-protocol-*.log file (not the main server log) so two independent writers never append to the same file concurrently, and gated by its own --protocol-log-level rather than the main --log-level — the two are deliberately decoupled (F41 follow-up): a user turning up file logging for our own app-level diagnosis shouldn't also flood their editor's Output panel with library internals, and vice versa.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Logging.ProtocolLoggerProvider.#ctor(System.String,System.Diagnostics.TraceLevel)
member.summary: Creates the provider, building the default protocol-log file/debug sink for the given client IDE and log level.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Logging.ProtocolLoggerProvider.#ctor(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Logging.IIdeSupportLogger)
member.summary: Test seam: bypasses the real file/debug sinks.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Performance.IOperationDurationRecorder
member.summary: Records the wall-clock duration of an LSP protocol operation for the architecture's Performance Verification "Layer 4" field instrumentation: real-world P95 measured in the live server, emitted via the existing logging path and (optionally, sampled) as a telemetry metric.
member.remarks: This is the single cross-cutting sink invoked at each handler boundary. It exists because the four interactive performance targets live on three different registration rails — manual OnRequest delegates (semanticTokens/full), OmniSharp AddHandler handlers (completion, definition) and a MediatR notification push (publishDiagnostics) — so no single MediatR pipeline behavior can cover them all.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Performance.IOperationDurationRecorder.Measure(System.String,OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.DocumentUri)
member.summary: Starts a timing scope; the elapsed time is recorded when the returned handle is disposed. Usage: using var _ = recorder.Measure("textDocument/completion", uri);

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Performance.IOperationDurationRecorder.Record(System.String,System.Double,OmniSharp.Extensions.LanguageServer.Protocol.DocumentUri)
member.summary: Records an already-measured duration for . Use this overload when the operation label is only known after the work runs (e.g. keyword vs. step completion).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Performance.IPerfTelemetrySampler
member.summary: Decides whether a given PerfSample telemetry event should be emitted. Field perf instrumentation fires on every interactive request, so the telemetry metric is sampled to bound event volume — the local log line (always written at verbose) is the unsampled record.

member.summary: Gets or sets the should sample.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Performance.PerfTelemetrySampler
member.summary: Probabilistic sampler. The rate is read from the REQNROLL_PERF_TELEMETRY_SAMPLE environment variable (a fraction in [0,1]); when unset or unparsable it defaults to = 0, i.e. perf telemetry is opt-in. Set it to e.g. 0.05 to emit ~5% of samples. The host-side opt-out gate still applies downstream.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler
member.summary: Handles by re-parsing feature files that belong to the affected project, then publishing a for each open file so that semantic tokens are refreshed against the new binding registry.

remarks.para: When is (startup, post-build connector run, or membership-baseline arrival), all feature files owned by the project are scanned — including files not currently open in the editor — so that the binding match cache is workspace-complete for Find Step Definition Usages / Find All References. The file list is obtained from the membership index (I1) when a baseline has been received; otherwise it falls back to a folder glob for backwards compatibility with clients that do not send reqnroll/projectFiles.
When is (incremental Roslyn per-file patch on a .cs edit), only the currently open feature files owned by the project are re-parsed.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler.Handle(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets or sets the handle.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler.RemoveBindingFilesAsync(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{System.String},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Purges the given binding-role .cs files from 's registry (issue #94). Called for files removed via a reqnroll/projectFiles delta -- e.g. the user deletes a step-definition file in the IDE. Visual Studio never sends workspace/didChangeWatchedFiles for this, so 's deletion path (the one place removal was previously wired up) never fires for it; this project-files-driven removal is what actually reaches VS. Uses (not RemoveFileAsync) because the membership index has already dropped the file by the time this runs, so owner-resolution would find nothing to remove; parsing empty text against the already-known project replaces the file's stale entries with none.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler.RequestCodeLensRefreshAsync(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject)
member.summary: Asks the client to re-pull C# step code lenses after a full registry replacement. Visual Studio cannot route the standard workspace/codeLens/refresh request to our pipe-based code-lens provider (the VS client's StepCodeLensService uses the LspInterceptingPipe directly, not VS's built-in LSP code-lens infrastructure), so for VS we push a custom reqnroll/refreshCodeLens notification the client intercepts to invalidate its rendered lenses. VS Code / Rider use the standard request.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler.RediscoverCsFilesAsync(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
After a Connector full replacement (which loads bindings from the compiled assembly), re-runs Roslyn source-level discovery on the project's .cs step-definition files so that source edited since the last build overrides the stale compiled bindings.
Covers two cases:
open, possibly-unsaved .cs buffers — reconciled unconditionally, since an unsaved edit is never reflected in the DLL; and
closed .cs files on disk whose last-write time is newer than the output assembly — i.e. edited then saved without a rebuild. This is the case that survives a VS restart: the file is on disk but not open, so without this it would never override the stale compiled binding.
Files unchanged since the build are faithfully represented by the DLL and are skipped to bound the cost. Reconciliation is delegated to , which patches the project's registry directly without consulting the membership index (the baseline may not have arrived yet at startup).

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler.CollectCsFilesToReconcile(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject)
member.summary: Selects the .cs files to reconcile after a full replacement and pairs each with the source text to parse: every open project-owned .cs buffer (unsaved edits always win, using the buffer text), plus closed step-definition files newer than the compiled assembly (using on-disk text).

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedHandler.EnumerateProjectStepDefinitionFiles(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject)
member.summary: Enumerates the project's .cs step-definition files: the membership index when a baseline has been received (authoritative — includes linked files, excludes obj/bin), otherwise a folder glob that skips build output.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedNotification
member.summary: Published when a project's is replaced after a successful connector discovery run (e.g. triggered by a build or a reqnroll.json change).
member.remarks: When is (e.g. startup or a post-build reflection discovery run), consumers should re-parse all workspace feature files that belong to — not only the currently open ones — so that the binding match cache covers the complete workspace for features such as Find Step Definition Usages / Find All References. When is (incremental Roslyn re-discovery on a .cs edit), re-parsing only the open feature files is sufficient immediately, and closed feature files are additionally rescanned after a debounce so their cached usage counts stay correct without waiting for a rebuild. This notification is only published for an incremental patch when it actually changed a binding's matched expression -- a patch that didn't (e.g. a method-body edit) never reaches here, since there is nothing to re-parse.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.BindingRegistryChangedNotification.#ctor(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject,System.Boolean,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{System.String})
member.summary: Published when a project's is replaced after a successful connector discovery run (e.g. triggered by a build or a reqnroll.json change).
member.remarks: When is (e.g. startup or a post-build reflection discovery run), consumers should re-parse all workspace feature files that belong to — not only the currently open ones — so that the binding match cache covers the complete workspace for features such as Find Step Definition Usages / Find All References. When is (incremental Roslyn re-discovery on a .cs edit), re-parsing only the open feature files is sufficient immediately, and closed feature files are additionally rescanned after a debounce so their cached usage counts stay correct without waiting for a rebuild. This notification is only published for an incremental patch when it actually changed a binding's matched expression -- a patch that didn't (e.g. a method-body edit) never reaches here, since there is nothing to re-parse.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.DiagnosticsPublishHandler
member.summary: Handles by aggregating the current parse-error tags and binding-mismatch data for the affected feature file and pushing a textDocument/publishDiagnostics notification to the IDE client.
member.remarks:
Fires alongside and on every . No ordering guarantee between these handlers is required — they are independent.
The LSP specification requires that a single publishDiagnostics message delivers the complete diagnostic set for a URI; sending a partial set clears the diagnostics not included. The combines both sources (parse errors and binding mismatches) into one list before this handler sends the single push.
For shared/linked features the primary owner's match set is used (primary-owner resolution / shared-feature scoping 2A/2B: LSP provides one result per URI with no project context, so one owner must be chosen).

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Schedules to run for after the debounce window elapses. A call for the same project before the window elapses cancels the pending run and restarts the window, so only the last scheduled action for a project actually runs.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.MatchCacheChangedNotification
member.summary: Published after a feature document has been (re)parsed and its binding matches recomputed and stored in — i.e. whenever the match cache for changes, whether triggered by a text edit, a binding-registry replacement, or a configuration change.
member.remarks:
This is the MatchCacheChangedNotification of section 3 / section 6 of the LSP IDE Support design. Consumers re-read the current tags / match set rather than receiving them
asks the client to refresh semantic tokens, and the (future) diagnostics aggregator pushes textDocument/publishDiagnostics.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.SemanticTokensPushHandler
member.summary: Pushes encoded semantic tokens to the Visual Studio client whenever a feature file's match cache changes.
member.remarks: Visual Studio's built-in LSP semantic-token colorizer maps token-type names through a fixed internal table (so it cannot honour Reqnroll's custom reqnroll.* types) and only pulls tokens lazily and unreliably. For VS we therefore push the tokens proactively via the custom reqnroll/semanticTokens notification; the VS client decodes them and colours the file with its own classifier. This handler is a no-op for every other client, which use the standard pull-based flow handled by .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.SemanticTokensPushHandler.Handle(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.MatchCacheChangedNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets or sets the handle.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Pipeline.SemanticTokensRefreshHandler
member.summary: Handles by asking the LSP client to refresh its semantic tokens. No tag encoding is performed here; encoding is deferred until the client sends a textDocument/semanticTokens/full request.
member.remarks: The refresh is driven by the match-cache notification (rather than a raw parse notification) so that it fires only after binding matches have been recomputed and the binding-overlay tags are current; refreshing earlier would re-encode pre-binding tokens. Multiple notifications can arrive in quick succession (e.g. when several files open at once, or a build replaces the registry). A debounce window collapses those bursts into a single workspace/semanticTokens/refresh request so the client is not flooded. The refresh is also guarded by a client-capability check: if the client did not advertise workspace.semanticTokens.refreshSupport the request is skipped.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Protocol.Documents.SourceLocationExtensions.ToLspLocation(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Core.Documents.SourceLocation)
member.summary: Converts a to an LSP for use in textDocument/definition responses. Always produces a zero-width range at the start position — consistent with LSP convention where the definition range identifies the symbol, not its body. End-position data from the discovery layer (e.g. PDB sequence-point spans from the Connector) is intentionally discarded here.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Protocol.Documents.SourceLocationExtensions.WithIdentifierLocation(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Core.Documents.SourceLocation,System.String)
member.summary:
Returns a refined to the method identifier token rather than the method body start.
The connector discovery path stores the first PDB sequence point — typically the opening { of the method body. This helper reads a small backward window in the source file to locate the line that contains the method name, which is what the Roslyn discovery path reports and what produces a useful "Code" column in the VS declarations window. When the file is inaccessible or the name cannot be found the original location is returned unchanged.
member.param: The location to refine (typically pointing at {).
The qualified method name from the binding, e.g. "CalculatorSteps.AddNumbers(int, int)". Only the simple name after the last . (and before any () is used for the search.

member.summary: Singleton registered in the DI container. Owns one per and routes registry lookups to the provider that owns the requested document's project.

remarks.para: Subscribes to / so that per-project providers are created and torn down automatically as the IDE glue sends project notifications.
The per-project provider is also stored in [typeof(ConnectorBindingRegistryProvider)] so that can reach it for output-assembly change events without going through this singleton.

Registries are intentionally not merged: step definitions belong to a single project and a feature file should only be matched against the bindings of its own project. routes to the correct per-project registry via (primary-owner resolution / shared-feature scoping 2A: deterministic home-project rule; no nondeterminism from baseline-arrival order).
When any project's registry is replaced the router publishes a via MediatR so that open feature files belonging to that project are re-parsed and semantic tokens refreshed.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Gets or sets the dispose.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Registry.ConnectorBindingRegistryProvider
member.summary:
Per-project backed by .
One instance lives in (keyed by typeof(ConnectorBindingRegistryProvider)), created by when a project is discovered.
Discovery runs are debounced (500 ms) and cancellable: calling while a run is in flight cancels it and starts a fresh one. When a run succeeds the last-good registry is replaced atomically and is raised. When a run fails the last-good registry is kept and an error is logged; the registry is never replaced with after it has been successfully populated once.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Registry.ConnectorBindingRegistryProvider.#ctor(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.LspReqnrollProject,Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Logging.IIdeSupportLogger)
member.summary: Creates a provider backed by the default connector-based discovery service (generic/custom connector selected per project configuration).

member.summary: Creates a provider backed by a caller-supplied discovery service. Used by tests to substitute discovery so the debounce/cancellation/swap behaviour can be verified in isolation from the out-of-process connector.
Creates a provider backed by a caller-supplied discovery service and telemetry service.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Registry.ConnectorBindingRegistryProvider.TriggerRefresh
M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Registry.ConnectorBindingRegistryProvider.ApplyRoslynFileUpdateAsync(Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Core.Bindings.CSharpStepDefinitionFile)
member.summary: Applies an immediate, source-level (Roslyn) binding update for a single C# file on top of the current registry, replacing only that file's step definitions and hooks (Roslyn/C# source-level binding discovery).
member.remarks: This is the in-process counterpart to the out-of-process reflection connector: it gives instant feedback as the user edits a step-definition file, without waiting for a build. The patch is layered on top of and intentionally does not advance the connector's last-good hash, so the next successful connector run (after a real build, whose assembly hash differs) replaces the whole registry with the authoritative post-build result. If no build has happened, the connector run is a hash-match no-op and the Roslyn patch survives.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Registry.IProjectBindingRegistryLookup
member.summary: Resolves the for a specific document URI within the LSP server, routing the lookup to the that owns the project the document belongs to.
member.remarks:
This is the correct server-side abstraction for step-definition lookup. Unlike (which is a per-project interface), this interface is URI-aware so consumers do not need to know which project a document belongs to — that routing is done here via .
Registry-change notifications flow through MediatR as rather than through a C# event on this interface, so that all cross-cutting LSP concerns follow the same established notification pattern.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Telemetry.FileLoggingLspTelemetryService
member.summary:
Decorates to mirror every emitted event to a local JSONL debug log () before forwarding to the wrapped service.
The mirror is independent of the host transmission and the analytics opt-out, so it records exactly what the server produced even when the event is dropped downstream or telemetry is disabled. When the debug log is not configured the sink is a no-op and this decorator simply forwards.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.MembershipState
member.summary: Describes how a URI relates to the authoritative project-membership index populated by reqnroll/projectFiles notifications.

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.MembershipState.Owned
member.summary: At least one project has claimed the file in a received baseline payload. Binding-dependent features (step matching, diagnostics, Go-to-Definition) are available.

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.MembershipState.Pending
member.summary: No project has claimed the file yet, but at least one project that could cover this path has not sent a baseline. The server treats the file as best-effort: folder-prefix fallback is used so startup does not regress until baselines arrive.

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.MembershipState.Unowned
member.summary: No project claims the file and every covering project has already sent its baseline. The file is intentionally excluded from all projects. Binding-dependent features are suppressed to avoid phantom diagnostics (invariant I2).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.ProjectKey
member.summary: Identifies a (project, TFM) combination in the membership index. Phase 1 matches on only; is stored for a future per-TFM keying follow-up.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.ProjectKey.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Identifies a (project, TFM) combination in the membership index. Phase 1 matches on only; is stored for a future per-TFM keying follow-up.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.LSP.Server.Workspace.WatchedFilesHandler
member.summary:
Handles workspace/didChangeWatchedFiles notifications.
reqnroll.json changes Reload the owning project's configuration and trigger binding re-discovery.
.editorconfig changes Invalidate the EditorConfig cache and reload configuration for all projects in the affected workspace scope.
Output assembly changes (bin/**/*.dll) Trigger binding re-discovery for the project whose output path matches.
C# source file deletions (*.cs) Remove the deleted file's step definitions from the binding registry so that stale entries do not appear in Find Unused Step Definitions results.
Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Gets or sets the get registration options.

member.summary: Gets or sets the handle.
